payload.prompt:
Update Readme. I want the next information points to be added/updated.
1. Current Status. Mention that nearly all MVP features are complete, except for Facebook and Microsoft login, permanent storage of blacklists of invalidated JWT tokens, and basic statistical tracking.
2. In the Building section, I want a step-by-step description of generating a front-end client out of backend endpoints/requests/responses.
3. I want a detailed but concise description of the development environment, including a mention that Docker is used to host the DB and appropriate commands from the Makefile to run/stop the environment (do not repeat what is already in the Building section).
4. I want a detailed but concise description of the building artefacts to be deployed to the prod environment (maybe add it to the Building section or create a separate one).
5. I want a concise description of the versioning policy, mechanism and commands.

If you have any questions about this prompt, please ask before making any changes.

tool_input.explanation: Update README with requested status, API client generation steps, development environment, deploy artifacts, and versioning policy details.
tool_input.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /home/alexl70/src/pet/linkshortener/README.md
@@
-## Status
-
-⚠️ **This project is currently under construction.** The MVP has not been created yet. This is an ongoing development effort.
+## Current Status
+
+⚠️ **This project is still under construction, but the MVP is nearly complete.**
+
+Implemented or largely implemented:
+
+- OAuth login flow with Google
+- User URL management (create/list/update/delete)
+- Redirect flow and backend API contract generation
+- Development and production build/deploy tooling
+
+Still pending for MVP completion:
+
+- Microsoft OAuth login integration
+- Facebook OAuth login integration
+- Permanent storage for invalidated JWT blacklist entries
+- Basic statistical tracking for shortened URLs
@@
 ## Building
@@
 To regenerate SQL scripts without building:
@@
 make generate
 ```
+
+### Generate Frontend API Client from Backend Endpoints
+
+Use this workflow whenever backend endpoint request/response schemas change.
+
+1. Update backend handlers, viewmodels, and Huma route registrations.
+2. Regenerate the OpenAPI spec from backend routes:
+   ```bash
+   make generate-spec
+   ```
+3. Regenerate the typed frontend API client from the OpenAPI file:
+   ```bash
+   cd frontend && npm run generate:api
+   ```
+4. Validate that the generated client compiles with the frontend:
+   ```bash
+   cd frontend && npm run build
+   ```
+
+The generated client is written into `frontend/src/lib/api/` and should be committed together with backend API changes.
+
+## Development Environment
+
+The project runs as a split local environment:
+
+- PostgreSQL is hosted in Docker via `docker compose`.
+- Backend runs with live reload using Air.
+- Frontend runs with Vite dev server.
+
+Useful commands from the `Makefile`:
+
+- Start only PostgreSQL in Docker: `make run-pg-dev`
+- Start backend only: `make run-backend-dev`
+- Start frontend only: `make run-frontend-dev`
+- Start full local environment: `make run-dev`
+- Stop full local environment: `make stop-dev`
+- Stop services individually: `make stop-pg-dev`, `make stop-backend-dev`, `make stop-frontend-dev`
+
+## Production Build Artifacts
+
+For production deployment, the repository builds and ships these artifacts:
+
+- Backend executable: `bin/server`
+  - Built by `make build-backend-prod` (linux/amd64, with stripped symbols).
+- Frontend static bundle: `frontend/dist/`
+  - Built by `make build-frontend-prod APP_BASE_URL=https://yourdomain.com`.
+- Full production bundle: both of the above via `make build-prod APP_BASE_URL=https://yourdomain.com`.
+- Database migration SQL scripts in `sql/`
+  - Regenerated by `make build` (via `make generate`) and used for production schema rollout.
+
+The `make deploy` target builds production artifacts, syncs `bin/server` and `frontend/dist/` to the server, and restarts the systemd service.
+
+## Versioning
+
+This project uses **Semantic Versioning** (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`) stored in the root `version` file.
+
+- Policy:
+  - `MAJOR`: breaking changes
+  - `MINOR`: backward-compatible feature additions
+  - `PATCH`: backward-compatible fixes
+- Mechanism:
+  - The current version is embedded into backend builds via Go linker flags.
+  - Release tags are created as `vX.Y.Z`.
+- Command:
+  - `make bump BUMP=patch` (or `minor`, `major`)
+
+`make bump` updates the `version` file, creates a commit, and creates the corresponding git tag.
*** End Patch

tool_input.explanation: Update Deploy.md: add note about automatic grants in Step 4, and add chmod 755 to the primary Caddyfile install command in Step 5.

replacements.oldString:
### Step 4 — Apply the database schema

From your **local machine**:

```bash
cat sql/schema.sql | ssh user@your-server "sudo -u postgres psql linkshortener"
```
replacements.newString:
### Step 4 — Apply the database schema

From your **local machine**:

```bash
cat sql/schema.sql | ssh user@your-server "sudo -u postgres psql linkshortener"
```

> **Note:** `schema.sql` includes a conditional `GRANT` block at the end that automatically grants `SELECT`, `INSERT`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE` on all tables and `USAGE`, `SELECT` on all sequences to the `linkshortener` database role. It also sets `ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES` so future migration SQL applied as the `postgres` superuser will automatically grant the same access. No manual `GRANT` statements are needed.

replacements.oldString:
```bash
scp deploy/Caddyfile user@your-server:/tmp/Caddyfile
ssh user@your-server "sudo cp /tmp/Caddyfile /etc/caddy/Caddyfile && sudo systemctl reload caddy"
```
replacements.newString:
```bash
scp deploy/Caddyfile user@your-server:/tmp/Caddyfile
ssh user@your-server "sudo cp /tmp/Caddyfile /etc/caddy/Caddyfile && sudo chmod 755 /opt/linkshortener && sudo systemctl reload caddy"
```

> **Note:** `chmod 755` makes `/opt/linkshortener` world-traversable so Caddy (which runs as the `caddy` system user) can read the static frontend files. The application binary is not affected.
